title: Astrobotic says sale to Voyager will allow it to scale up
summary: Astrobotic will sell to Voyager Technologies to accelerate scaling for NASA's lunar base initiative. The acquisition aims to meet projected demands in lunar lander development.

title: India’s Jio lays out sovereign LEO constellation plan ahead of IPO
summary: Jio Platforms plans to build a sovereign LEO satellite constellation in India by leasing broadband capacity from existing constellations. The move is part of its strategy ahead of an IPO.

title: MDA Space to buy Blue Canyon Technologies to gain foothold in U.S. market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring smallsat manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies for $620 million to expand its presence in the U.S. government market. The deal aims to leverage BCT's expertise in small satellites to strengthen MDA Space's competitive position.

title: ElevationSpace Secures US $40 Million in Series B Funding, Bringing Total Raised to US $63.5 Million
summary: ElevationSpace raised an additional $40 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million. The company focuses on space-to-Earth transportation and a space environment utilization and recovery platform.

title: Northrop Grumman says industry ready to scale solid rocket production, with longer contracts
summary: Northrop Grumman states the space industry is prepared to scale up solid rocket production, contingent on longer contracts. The company emphasizes the need for predictable demand from suppliers despite billions in private investment.

title: The MEO durability crisis: why LEO hardware will fail the new orbital economy
summary: The article discusses the challenges of Medium Earth Orbit (MEO) durability compared to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) hardware, highlighting why LEO solutions may not meet the demands of the emerging orbital economy.

title: NASA selects mission to study space weather interaction with Earth’s atmosphere
summary: NASA has chosen a new space science mission to investigate how space weather affects Earth's atmosphere. The mission aims to enhance understanding of this interaction.

title: Chinese university-led mission to study asteroid Apophis during close encounter with Earth
summary: A spacecraft from Tsinghua University will participate in international efforts to study asteroid Apophis during its close approach to Earth in 2029. This mission aims to gather scientific data about the asteroid's composition and behavior.

title: Mu-g Technologies enters the parabolic flight business
summary: Mu-g Technologies has acquired an aircraft to offer commercial parabolic flight services. This move comes as NASA seeks to acquire its own aircraft for reduced-gravity research.

title: Austrian propulsion startup joins sovereign space funding surge
summary: Austrian satellite propulsion startup Gate Space secured 6.3 million euros in funding from Europe's government-backed accelerator program, contributing to Europe's push for greater space sovereignty.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches 24 Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit from California
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched 24 Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California. This marks another successful deployment in SpaceX's Starlink constellation.

title: A mysterious gamma-ray stream comes from the Milky Way's center. Could dark matter have something to do with it?
summary: A new study suggests that self-annihilating dark matter could be the source of a mysterious gamma-ray emission from the Milky Way's center, known as the Galactic Center Excess. The research has not ruled out this possibility despite ongoing debate.

title: Happy Summer Solstice! Today marks the longest day of the year for the Northern Hemisphere
summary: Today marks the summer solstice, the longest day of the year for the Northern Hemisphere, signaling the start of astronomical summer.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 215 — Disclosure Day
summary: Episode 215 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with Dr. David Brin, a bestselling sci-fi/sci-fact author, hosted by Rod Pyle and Susan Karlin. The episode focuses on topics related to space and science fiction.

title: Supermassive black holes may be surrounded by dark matter clusters, new 'echo map' technique suggests
summary: A new 'echo map' technique suggests supermassive black holes may be surrounded by dark matter clusters. This includes the black hole at the heart of the Milky Way.

title: Do we need a lunar building code to build moon bases safely?
summary: As NASA aims to establish a lasting human presence on the moon, an expert suggests the need for a lunar building code to ensure safe construction of moon bases. This highlights the challenges of adapting terrestrial construction standards to the lunar environment.

title: Late, great singer-songwriter Elliott Smith finds immortality as an asteroid
summary: An asteroid has been named after late singer-songwriter Elliott Smith, who passed away in 2003 at the age of 34. This marks a unique tribute to his legacy in the cosmos.

title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
summary: A California-based private company will design, build, and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 for the Aeolus mission. The spacecraft will be the first to provide daily measurements of Mars' global environment.

title: 'This is not like life that we’ve experienced' 'Star City' cast on struggling to relate to the brutality of Soviet existence in Apple TV's For All Mankind' spin-off
summary: Apple TV+'s 'Star City' spin-off from 'For All Mankind' explores the harsh realities of Soviet life, which cast members find difficult to relate to. The series delves into the brutal existence experienced during that era.

title: The most unpredictable meteor shower of the year peaks next week. Here's what to expect
summary: The June Bootids meteor shower, known for its unpredictability, is set to peak next week with potential surprise outbursts despite typically producing few meteors.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4920-4926: Surveying the Bands
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is surveying distinct rock layers with varying textures and tones as it ascends Mount Sharp. The mission involves analyzing these geological bands to understand the planet's past.

title: NASA Mission to Study Space Weather Impacts of Earth’s Atmosphere
summary: NASA selected the DAPHNE mission to study space weather impacts on Earth's atmosphere and improve prediction capabilities for technology like GPS and satellites. The mission will advance to Phase B of development.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition
summary: NASA has awarded contracts to eight new companies and will acquire data from six existing holders to expand commercial satellite data availability for research and decision-making. The new data will supplement NASA's Earth satellites with high-resolution and frequent observations.

title: From Suriname to Space: Rohit Goeptar Shares His Journey to NASA
summary: Rohit Goeptar shares his inspiring journey from a poor family in Suriname to working at NASA. His story highlights resilience and the pursuit of space exploration despite early hardships.

title: Desert Field Test With NASA Advanced Rover Prototype
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory tested the ERNEST rover, an advanced four-wheel prototype with enhanced mobility and robotic autonomy, in the Colorado Desert near Plaster City, California, in March 2026.

title: NASA Testing Advanced Capabilities for Moon, Mars Rovers
summary: NASA is testing the ERNEST rover in California's Colorado Desert to advance robotic autonomy for future Moon and Mars missions. The prototype has traveled 16 miles with minimal human intervention.

title: NASA’s Lucy Reveals Wobbling, Peanut-Shaped Asteroid
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered that the asteroid Donaldjohanson has a wobbly, peanut-like shape and a complex history shaped by a violent collision 155 million years ago.

title: Stages of Star Formation
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image showing various stages of star formation in the Orion Molecular Cloud. The image highlights stellar embryos and protoplanetary discs in a cold gas and dust filament.

title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a merging galaxy cluster, CL0016+1609 or MACS J0018.5+1626, which is bright at X-ray wavelengths and has been extensively studied. X-ray observations revealed that the cluster is composed of two merging clusters along the line of sight.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of operations aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing toward the 2026 debut of its Terran R heavy-lift vehicle after shipping the second stage to Stennis Space Center. The development marks a key milestone in the company's efforts to compete in the commercial launch market.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is headquartered in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Both are key components in SpaceX's development program.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for a single internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its first launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification of the first stage of its Nova rocket, verifying its structural integrity. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). This crewed mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for versatile applications in the space industry. This innovation aims to enhance automation and efficiency in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring the durability of materials in the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas from these researchers.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on resilient or advanced technology in the space industry, though specific details are not provided.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute test underwent a critical 'bake-out' process to ensure its reliability. The procedure likely involved thermal testing to validate the parachute's performance under extreme conditions.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated high-fidelity quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite. The company plans to launch the satellite by 2027.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, achieving all test objectives and being fully commissioned. The mission marks a significant milestone for the company's autonomous space capabilities.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly participate in lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst secured $12 million in funding to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO), with plans for a 2027 mission. The company will first launch a mission closer to Earth this month.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace secured $25 million in a Series B funding round to advance its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration. The New Zealand-Dutch company aims to accelerate development with the new capital.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with the Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) to develop AI-based anomaly detection technology, currently being tested on terrestrial flat sats with potential expansion into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a broad decline in other publicly traded space companies' stocks. Investors pulled back from major players across the sector.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract awarded on June 18. The company aims to showcase in-space servicing capabilities for national security applications.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has partnered with Iceye to use satellite data for flood monitoring. This collaboration leverages Iceye's advanced satellite capabilities to enhance disaster response in Norway.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall are forming a joint venture to develop sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The partnership was announced through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The Stockholm-based firm announced the acquisition on June 18.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. CEO Eric Sung described the facility as a 'defining milestone' for Intellian.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a recently formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to enhance its satellite offerings. The acquisition follows Lyntris' formation through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund, led by Jeff Bezos, is investing $26 million to support the development of the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite system. The investment is part of a broader coalition backing the non-profit initiative.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the satellite industry. It also mentions some breaking news related to the conversation.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying it to prioritize space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following a recent U.S. Space policy shift.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate unveiled new space cybersecurity research under the SPARTA framework to safeguard critical infrastructure. The initiative aims to enhance protection against cyber threats in the space sector.
